converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Charles G. Muller papers
Muller, Charles Geoffrey, 1897-1987.
3788
14.5 cubic feet. 14.5 cubic feet.
1914-1986. Correspondence, photographs, scrapbooks, pamphlets, notes, and audio recordings. Various items documenting the life and work of Charles G. Muller. Collection material in English 2070080
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
S. Lee, M.E. Warren
Date completed:
January, 1992
EAD encoding:
Julia Lonnberg and Peter Martinez, October 2001
Date modified:
RMC Staff, July 2012
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Papers of Charles G. Muller, include diaries (1914-1921) and appointment books (1922-1973) that relate to Muller's activities and attitudes; correspondence, photographs, and scrapbooks concerning his service with an American Red Cross ambulance unit in France (1917) and a trip he took around the world (1919-1920), the latter including Red Cross work in Libau, Latvia; research files for books on the War of 1812, THE PROUDEST DAY: MACDONOUGH ON LAKE CHAMPLAIN (1960) and THE DARKEST DAY: 1814, THE WASHINGTON-BALTIMORE CAMPAIGN (1963); pamphlets, notes, address lists, correspondence, and recorded speeches pertaining to his activities with Alcoholics Anonymous, Alateen, and the National Council on Alcoholism; notes and drafts of NO CORK FOR THE BOTTLE; documentation on many consulting projects, primarily in the field of fund raising; notes, drafts, correspondence, and manuscript for DEMOCRACY'S GIFT DOLLARS; and correspondence, drafts, and notes pertaining to other writing jobs. Also, material relating to Cornell University, and Muller's research material for a March 1984 CORNELL ALUMNI NEWS article "Scotty's Gold Mine," detailing the partnership of Walter Edward Scott (Death Valley Scotty) and Albert Mussey and Bessie Penniman Johnson, and their Death Valley estate, Scotty's Castle.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Diaries and Datebooks, 1914-1986
Box 1
Series II. Scrapbooks, Memorabilia and Correspondence
Box 2
Series III. War of 1812 Research Materials
Boxes 3-4
Series IV. Alcoholics Anonymous
Boxes 4-6, 10
Series V. Writing Jobs
Boxes 6-9, 15-19
Series VI. Miscellany
Boxes 10-14
Cite As:
Charles G. Muller Papers, #3788. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Journalist, author, consultant. Cornell University Class of 1918. Charles G. Muller died in 1987. In the Cornellian and in Cornell alumni records, his name is given as Charles George Muller; but as an author, he styled himself Charles Geoffrey Muller or Charles G. Muller.
Speeches. Photographs. Diaries. United States -- History -- War of 1812. Liepāja (Latvia). Death Valley (Calif.) Journalists. Authors. World War, 1914-1918. Fund raising. Alcoholism. Johnson, Albert Mussey. Johnson, Bessie Penniman. Muller, Charles Geoffrey, 1897-1987. (Title of work: Darkest day: 1814..) Muller, Charles Geoffrey, 1897-1987. (Title of work: No cork for the bottle..) Muller, Charles Geoffrey, 1897-1987. (Title of work: Proudest day..) Scott, Walter Edward. Brakeley, George A. (Title of work: Democracy\'s gift dollars..) Death Valley Scotty. Al-Anon Family Group Headquarters, Inc Alcoholics Anonymous National Council on Alcoholism American National Red Cross Scotty\'s Castle
2070080 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Diaries and Datebooks, 1914-1986"My Trip Abroad"1914Records events on his trip to England, Scotland, Belgium, Holland, Germany, Switzerland, and Italy.11"Datebook for 1917"1917(no entries Jan. 5-May 16, Oct. 9 Dec. 31) Records activities while in Section 22, Norton Harjes American Red Cross Ambulance Service with the French Army.12"Datebook for 1918"1918(no entries Jan. 1-May 20) Records drive west from Ithaca to Indiana to enlist, back to Syracuse and Ithaca, working at Treman King?s, early writing pursuits, training and service as an aviation pilot with the U.S. Naval Reserve.13"Datebook for 1919"1919Contains accounts of activities in Pensacola, Florida, New York City, Ithaca, and at summer camp and travels in England, France, Spain and St. Nazaire.14"Agenda de Bureau pour 1920"1920Records remainder of stay in Libau for the American Red Cross Commission to Western Russia and travels to Riga, Norway, London, the Antwerp Olympics, Nantes, the Red Sea, the Indian Ocean, China, Vietnam, Japan, Hawaii, California and East to Boston.15"Daily Reminder 1921"1921(sporadic and usually brief entries) In New York, Ithaca and the mid-west, settled in Michigan where he was reporter and editor for the Grand Rapids Press. book lists activities1966138Appointment book1966139Appointment book lists activities1967140Appointment book1967141Appointment book1968142Appointment book lists activities1968143Appointment book lists activities1969144Appointment book lists activities1970145Appointment book lists activities1971146Appointment book lists activities1972147Appointment book lists activities148Miscellaneous papers removed from diaries.Map of Pisa, postcards, and "Fleeing from the Wrath of Europe", a pamphlet by Mrs. Charles Wentworth (1914); a postcard of a water color, pocket calendar, and a list of authors and their works (1917); photo of troops and a camp and a hotel bill (1920) from the 1918 diary; plan of Toledo, and a postcard of peasants making planks (1919)149Address book (no date)150Appointment books1974-1986151Series II. Scrapbooks, memorabilia and correspondenceCornell scrapbook1916-1917Includes photos of people and buildings, pamphlets and programs. A number of items on baseball.2Photo album of American Red Cross section 22 with the French Army on the Verdun Front (1917) with key1917Includes pictures of the trip over, Paris, enroute to Verdun, just behind the lines, time off, and the trip home. Also, key to photo album.21Journal of Muller?s trip around the world in 1920Contains visiting cards, a typed journal (Sept. 8 ? Dec. 7, 1920), a story about Peking, "Adrian Acquires Confucius", written in Grand Rapids, "Peking Nights at the Play", vignettes from his trip, and his ship?s itinerary and passenger list.22"A Trip thru Europe during the summer of 1914"1914Collection of letters sent to his parents, George and Jennie (Hodgkinson) Muller from Europe.23"With the Red Cross in Russia. 1919-1920"‡1919-1921Documents, letters, journal entries and the Red Cross Bulletin, miscellaneous issues, June ? Nov. 1921.24"Grand Rapids Press"Volume contains stories, letters to Charlie and Georgi (Georgia L. Stafroogh) Muller, whom Charlie married in 1921, signed "Kiss". n.d.25"Around the World", Aug. 1919 ? Dec. 19201919-1921Photographs of France, Spain (LaCorida, Toledo), Russia (Libau, Civilian Relief work, Riga), Reine and a few related documents and clippings.26Photograph albumContains pictures of Sweden and Norway (Bergen), Antwerp, Belgium and the Olympic games, friends, Port Said, the Suez Canal, Djibute, Colombo, Singapore, Saigon, Hong-Kong, Shanghai, Puchow, Peking, the Great Wall, Mukden, Korea (Seoul), Japan (Kioto, Mara, Nikko, Kamakura), Honolulu, San Francisco, and Mexico.27DocumentsU.S. Passport, Red Cross i.d., map of the Front at Verdun, order for transport, two photographs (Muller in 1917), and miscellaneous documents related to Red Cross work.28World War I photographs29Photographs210U.S. Navy items211Series III.
